message:
fix+feat(trace,lab): correct gf2 column_pivot, add H1 persistence API, refactor explorer scoring

Three related fixes to the persistent homology pipeline used by the
deterministic chaos lab and the topology explorer:

src/trace/gf2.rs — column_pivot uses highest_bit (lowest is wrong):
  In the standard column-reduction algorithm for persistent homology
  on GF(2), the *pivot* of a column is the row index of the *highest*
  set bit, not the lowest.  The previous implementation called
  Column::pivot() (which returns the lowest bit), making column
  reductions match on the wrong row and producing incorrect
  persistence pairs.  Now uses Column::highest_bit().  Updated the
  in-file unit test (`test_persistence_pairs_simple`) which had been
  asserting the wrong-but-self-consistent prior behaviour: a single
  edge {v0, v1} reduces to a pair where the killed cell is v1 (the
  higher row), not v0.

src/trace/boundary.rs — add H1 persistence pairs API:
  The previous reduction reduced only the d2 (square) boundary, which
  cannot distinguish 1-cycles that get killed (born on edges, killed
  on squares) from 0-cycles that get killed (born on vertices, killed
  on edges).  Two new methods on SquareComplex:

  - combined_boundary_matrix(): builds the full filtration boundary
    matrix in dimension order — vertices first, then edges, then
    squares — so a single reduction yields all dimensions.

  - h1_persistence_pairs(): runs the full reduction and filters
    output to keep only:
      * pairs where birth ∈ edge column range AND death ∈ square
        column range
      * unpaired births in the edge column range (1-cycles that
        persist to infinity)

  Two regression tests added:
  - triangle_cycle_survives_as_unpaired_h1_class: an unfilled
    triangle should produce exactly one unpaired H1 class on an
    edge column.
  - h1_cycle_born_and_killed: rewrites the existing test to use
    the new API instead of hand-rolling the boundary matrix, and
    adds a stronger assertion that the unpaired list is empty
    (the square should kill the only H1 class).

src/lab/explorer.rs — extract score_trace_topology helper:
  TopologyExplorer::on_outcome had ~30 lines of inline boundary
  matrix construction and reduction, including the now-buggy approach
  of reducing d2 alone.  Extract to score_trace_topology() which
  delegates to the new h1_persistence_pairs() API and handles the
  empty-trace case by returning a ledger with empty pairs.  Added
  topology_scoring_detects_unfilled_triangle_h1_class regression
  test that builds a 3-event trace and verifies the resulting ledger
  contains exactly one H1 class with the expected birth column range.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
